own_review.issue: Core verification: the flipped boolean is the 5th arg (optional) of the 6-arg MetadataFactory.createRequirement(namespace, name, range, filter, optional, multiple) overload, which internally constructs RequiredCapability with min=optional?0:1, max=1, greedy=true. Greedy stays true, so the requirement is still pulled into the slice when the referenced bundle IS resolvable, and resolution simply no longer fails when it is not (e.g. environment-filtered SWT fragments). The one-boolean fix is therefore safe and complete; both jars.extra.classpath and per-jar jar.X..extra.classpath paths funnel through createRequirementFromPlatformURL, so the PR title's full scope is covered by this single change.
own_review.severity: info (correctness confirmation; greedy default is the key safety argument)

own_review.issue: Behavioral trade-off: with optional=true, a typo'd or genuinely-missing extra.classpath entry is now silently ignored instead of failing the build, and no warning is logged anywhere. This matches PDE semantics (the stated goal) but degrades diagnosability; a debug/warn log when an entry cannot be matched would help.

No test accompanies the change. The existing integration test only builds projects whose extra classpath entries all resolve (verifyErrorFreeLog on valid references); there is no coverage for the new unresolvable-entry-is-ignored behavior, the very scenario this PR exists for. The only reproducer is external (eclipse.platform.swt PR 1638).
The StatusTool import reorder is cosmetic; it actually restores alphabetical order (org.eclipse.tycho.core.shared sorts after org.eclipse.tycho.TychoConstants), so it is an improvement, not noise.

points.point: Correct identification of the 5th param (optional) of the 6-arg createRequirement overload, cross-verified against the tycho-core BundleDependenciesAction.createRequirement wrapper at lines 162-168 which has explicit /* optional */ and /* greedy */ comments — citation verified exact at base commit

OptionalResolutionAction flows to getPublisherActions() but not to getPublisherAdvice() where getExtraEntriesAdvice() is invoked (verified: P2GeneratorImpl.getPublisherAdvice line 407 calls getExtraEntriesAdvice without optionalAction), so extra classpath entries are always optional regardless of configuration — accurate, non-obvious design observation

Missing test for the unresolvable-optional-entry scenario; claims existing test only covers resolvable entries — verified true (ExtraClassPathEntriesTest only builds projects with valid entries), though v5 misnames it 'ExtraClasspathEntriesTest.java'
Cosmetic import reorder to alphabetical order, no functional impact — accurate but trivial

missed: Human's key insight: the 6-arg createRequirement overload already defaults greedy=true, so the requirement remains greedy and the fix is 'already as desired' — v5 never mentions greediness even though its own citation (BundleDependenciesAction with the true/* greedy */ comment) had the answer in view
exceeds: OptionalResolutionAction not reaching the extra-entries advice (design observation beyond the human review)
Missing test coverage for the unresolvable-entry scenario

points.point: Correct identification of the optional flag in the 6-arg createRequirement overload and its non-fatal-when-unresolvable semantics — accurate

Precedent analysis: P2ResolverImpl.optionalGreedyRequirementTo (line 364), InstallableUnitResolver optionalGreedySourceBundleRequirement (line 225), AdditionalBundleRequirementsInstallableUnitProvider (lines 78, 100) — ALL four citations verified exact at base commit, all use optional=true with greedy=true; this substantially captures the human's optional+greedy point via established convention

Import reorder flagged as noise with advice to 'preserve the original import order' — the observation is real but the advice is backwards: the new order is the correct alphabetical one, the original was out of order
Missing test: names the exact test class ExtraClassPathEntriesTest in tycho-its and correctly states it only covers resolvable entries; suggests a concrete unresolvable-entry test — verified accurate

exceeds: Precedent survey of optional+greedy createRequirement usage across four Tycho classes with exact, verified line numbers
Missing test coverage with exact test class name and concrete test design

qualitative: Both reviews are accurate on this small, subtle PR and both correctly judge the one-boolean fix sound; every checkable citation in both was verified real at the base commit, and both missing-test claims are true (zero absence-fabrications). The decisive difference is the human's key insight that the 6-arg createRequirement overload keeps greedy=true: v6 effectively covers it by citing four verified 'optionalGreedy' precedents (all optional=true, greedy=true) and arguing the change matches that convention, while v5 never engages with greediness at all despite quoting the very wrapper method whose 'true/* greedy */' comment spells it out. v5 compensates with a genuinely good extra finding (OptionalResolutionAction never reaches the extra-entries advice, verified at P2GeneratorImpl:407) that v6 lacks. v6's only blemish is mildly backwards advice to revert an import reorder that actually fixed alphabetical ordering, and it misnamed nothing — its line-number precision (git tools) clearly paid off. v6 edges out v5: 3 vs 2.
